Vigants-BarnettNorth American multipath model (Pathloss Eq.14–16).
ITU-R P.841Conversion between worst-month and average-annual statistics (Q₁=2.85, β=0.13). Used with P.530-19 when the worst-month → annual toggle is enabled.
ITU-R P.1144Bilinear interpolation method used for reading the 0.25° LogK and dN75 global grids at the path mid-point.

📡 Multipath reliability formulas

Rain outage uses the flat fade margin (FFM = RSLunfaded − RSLthreshold). Multipath outage uses the effective fade margin (EFM), which combines the flat and dispersive components:

EFM = −10·log₁₀(10^(−FFM/10) + 10^(−DFM/10))

MethodWorst-month multipath outage pw (%)
Vigants-Barnett P = 6×10⁻⁷ · C · f · d³ · 10^(−EFM/10) × 100
C = 0.25 (mountainous/dry) · 1.0 (average) · 4.0 (over-water/coastal).
Auto-C: C = cf · (S/15.2)^−1.3, where S = terrain roughness (6–42 m) from profile, cf = climate factor (0.5 dry, 1.0 avg, 2.0 humid).
ITU-R P.530-7/8
Inland		 K · d³ · f^0.89 · (1+|εp|)^−1.4 · 10^(−EFM/10)
K from Eq.29; d in km, f in GHz, εp in mrad.
ITU-R P.530-7/8
Coastal large water		 K from Eq.30: blends Ki (inland) and Kcl using over-water fraction rc.
Kcl = 2.3×10⁻⁴ · 10^(−0.1C₀ − 0.011|rc|)
ITU-R P.530-7/8
Coastal medium water		 K from Eq.31: blends Ki and Kcm using rc.
Kcm = geometric mean of Ki and Kcl.
ITU-R P.530-17 Same formula but with exponent d^3.6 instead of d^3.0 — more conservative for longer paths.
ITU-R P.530-19
(09/2025)		 p = K·d^3.51·(f²+13)^0.447·10^(−0.376·tanh((h₃−147)/125) −0.334·|εₚ|^0.39 −0.00027·hₗ +17.85·vₛₜ −EFM/10)
K = antilog of bilinear interpolation of LogK.csv at the path mid-point; vsr from dN75.csv (Eq.8/9); hc = (he+hr)/2 − d²/102 − ht (Eq.6); εp = |hr−he|/d (mrad); hL = lower antenna altitude (m ASL). For EFM below the transition fade At = 25 + 1.2·log₁₀p₀, the shallow-fading interpolation of §2.3.2 (Eq.12–18) is applied. Multipath outage is zero for paths ≤ 5 km (§2.3.1). Optional worst-month → annual conversion per ITU-R P.841 (Q₁=2.85, β=0.13), bounded 1 ≤ Q ≤ 12. Both ITU integral data grids are embedded (quantized to 8-bit: K within ±2.1%, dN75 within ±0.17 N-units).

Branching loss uses the direct-mount table for antennas ≤ 6 ft/1.8 m and the remote-mount table above that; the Miscellaneous-Loss field is added on top.

📚 Glossary

RSLReceived Signal Level. The RSL threshold is the minimum power the receiver needs for a given modulation / BER.
FSLFree-Space Loss — signal loss from spreading over distance.
FFMFlat Fade Margin — dB headroom against thermal noise fading.
DFMDispersive Fade Margin — equipment signature depth against selective fading.
EFMEffective Fade Margin — FFM and DFM combined (used for multipath outage).
ACMAdaptive Coding & Modulation — link automatically shifts to a more robust profile during fades.
C FactorVigants-Barnett terrain/climate factor: 0.25 (dry/rough) to 4.0 (flat/water).
KGeoclimatic factor in ITU-R P.530 — captures location-specific multipath severity.
εpPath inclination (mrad) — elevation angle between the two antenna ends.
STerrain roughness (m) — standard deviation of elevation samples along the path profile.
PL% of worst month with refractivity gradient <−100 N-units/km (ITU-R P.453).
WVDWater-Vapour Density (g/m³) — humidity term in atmospheric loss.
R₀.₀₁Rain rate (mm/h) exceeded 0.01 % of an average year.
AGLAbove Ground Level — antenna mounting height.
ASLAbove Sea Level — absolute altitude, used for geoclimatic K calculation.
Fresnel zoneElliptical region around the line of sight that must stay clear of obstacles (target: 60 % clearance).
AvailabilityPercentage of the year the link meets its performance target.
SDSpace Diversity — second receive antenna on the same tower, vertically offset, used to mitigate ground-reflected multipath on low-band (4–11 GHz) links.
I_SDSpace Diversity improvement factor — divisor applied to the multipath outage probability when SD is enabled (Vigants Eq. 23).
XPDCross-Polarisation Discrimination (dB) — measure of how well an antenna rejects the opposite polarisation. Higher = less co-channel interference.
XPICCross-Polarisation Interference Canceller — DSP device that cancels cross-polar interference. Provides XPIF dB of improvement.
XPIFXPIC Improvement Factor (dB) — product- and modulation-specific benefit of the XPIC device, from Ceragon test data.
C/ICarrier-to-Interference ratio (dB) — protection against cross-polar signal. Computed per modulation for multipath (Eq. 39/40) and rain (Eq. 42) conditions.
XPD₀Adjusted antenna XPD used in the co-channel calculation: XPDg+5 if XPDg≤35 dB, else 40 dB (ITU P.530-8 Eq. 33).
DMSDegrees-Minutes-Seconds coordinate format (e.g. 34° 22’ 49.2" N). The tool accepts decimal, DMS with symbols, DMS with spaces, or hemisphere prefix/suffix.
hcAntenna centre height above terrain (m) — average of the two antenna altitudes ASL minus the path mid-point terrain elevation. Used in P.530-19 multipath; clamped at 26 m minimum.
hLLower antenna altitude above sea level (m) — used in geoclimatic factor K calculation for P.530-17/19.
dN7575th-percentile refractivity gradient in the lowest 75 m of atmosphere — embedded 0.25° global grid from ITU-R P.530-19. Drives the vsr (vertical speed ratio) term.
vsrVertical speed ratio in P.530-19 — derived from dN75; typical range 0.8–3.5. Higher values increase predicted multipath outage.

📝 Revision history

Reverse chronological, one row per feature. v7.5, v7.9, v8.0, v8.1 and v8.2 are exact release dates; v7.6, v7.7 and v7.8 dates are approximate (they sit between v7.5 and v7.9, all in mid-June 2026).

VersionDateFeatureDescription
v8.22026-06-24LBC view-local toolbar above the mapNew horizontal toolbar at the top of the Site Location & Climate card consolidates File ▾ (Save / Load Design, Pathloss in / out, Add to Network, Export Excel, PDF Report), View ▾ (map background layers), Fetch Climate, Clear Link and Clear highlights — out of the global header where they overlapped with the Network view's own actions. The redundant Link distance badge was removed (the distance is already on the link line and in the budget panel). See Export & reporting and Site Location & Climate.
DMS coordinate input — compact two-row layoutLat row + Lon row per site, with the N/S and E/W hemisphere select inline at 54 px fixed width. Replaces the previous four-line wrap. Field IDs and parsers unchanged. See Site Location & Climate.
Network Report — multi-link planning reportNew report under Network → File ▾ → 📊 Network Report. Sortable HTML table (one row per link at the reference modulation), Compact / Standard / Detailed column presets plus custom mix, Excel two-sheet workbook, four-section landscape-A4 PDF. See Network Report.
Defaults — Distance-Banded Planning Rules upgradeBulk-import rules from Excel / CSV, live → Auto (cfg / mod) column, reference-modulation cap (256 QAM Microwave / 64 QAM mmW), one-screen layout polish. See Defaults — Distance-Banded Planning Rules.
Import LinksBulk-import draft links between site pairs from Excel / CSV under Network → File ▾ → Import Links. Existing site names reused; missing sites auto-created from row coordinates. See Network planning.
Visual polish — File menu order, card widths, button stylingGlobal header File ▾: KML interchange (Google Earth) section relocated to the bottom (just above Clear Network) so the libraries / sites / native file actions sit on top. Site Location & Climate card width now matches the Modulation Performance card above (stripped a redundant max-width:1340px wrapper). LBC toolbar buttons matched to the Network toolbar in height (padding:7px 14px) and text colour (color:#fff).
v8.12026-06-23Antenna RPE LibraryStandards-compliant NSMA WG 16.99.050 importer + polar viewer. Persisted to localStorage, accessible from File ▾. Drag-and-drop accepts both individual files and full folder trees (walked recursively).
ADF format supportDual parser handles both classical NSMA (bare-key, HH N sections) and the Andrew CommScope ADF variant (CSV-style KEY:,VALUE headers with PATCUT:,AZ sections). Auto-detection by content sniffing; works across .txt / .nsma / .adf / .ant extensions, case-insensitive.
Radio File LibraryPathloss V5 .raf importer. Reads Tx power, ATPC, Rx thresholds, per-modulation C/I + XPIF, Tx spectral mask (81 pts), Rx selectivity (81 pts), T/I curve (27 pts). Spec-type badging (Guarantee / Typical / Typical (derived)).
NF table + Derive TypicalEmbedded NF-table covering 5 NF families × standard bands. 🧪 Derive Typical button creates Typical entries from Guarantee entries by subtracting the per-band NF delta from every Rx threshold; mask, selectivity, T/I, C/I, XPIF, Tx power are preserved.
Product matcher (PPT-aligned)Auto-resolves the specific Ceragon product from the radio ID per Pathloss_Radio_Files_format_for_LBC: IP-20C, IP-20C-HP, RFU-D, RFU-D-HP, IP-50C, IP-50CX, IP-50E, IP-50EX(A) (covers EX + EXA), IP-50EX-P, IP-50EXA-P. Handles all dashed and dashless forms (IP50EXP / IP-50EX-P equally).
Interference engineFull per-link C/I analysis across the network. ITU-R F.758 Tx-max-for-interferer / Tx-nominal-for-victim, NFD from T/I curve, XPD with cross-pol penalty + NFD degradation, FSL + diffraction (P.526) + atmospheric (P.676 approx). Verdict thresholds 6 dB / 0 dB.
Auto-bindOne-click match of every designed link to its best radio + both antennas in the libraries, plus centre frequency on the BW raster and polarisations from the configuration (1+0/1+1 = V; 2+0/2+2/4+0 = V+H XPIC). Skips links with manualOverride flags.
Allocate Channels (DSATUR)Graph-coloring channel planner. Builds conflict graph from coordination radius + band overlap, runs DSATUR-greedy assignment scoring co-channel as ×100 and adjacent as ×5 with tie-break by spread. Respects pinned channels. Eliminates the band-centre co-channel collisions Auto-bind alone would create.
Per-link override modal⚙ Edit interference bindings button on the link popup opens a floating editor with dropdowns for radio / antenna A / antenna B / centre frequency and pol checkboxes per end. Each field has a Pinned / Auto button. ↺ Reset all to Auto rebuilds the link from scratch via the libraries.
Mitigation suggestions💡 Suggest fixes evaluates 6 candidates per problem link (swap pol A / B / both, shift ±1 channel, drop one modulation step) and reports predicted new margin. Apply commits the change, pins the touched field, and re-runs the network analysis.
Save / Load / PDFHeader actions on the results card: JSON snapshot with bindings + mitigations; load restores results without touching the network sites/links; PDF export produces a multi-page report via on-demand jsPDF (cover + per-problem-link detail + healthy appendix).
Result cache (50k LRU)Engine wraps _computeContribution with a memoizer keyed by iface fingerprint (radio + antennas + centre freq + pols + site coords + antAlt). Mitigation evaluation drops from minutes to seconds because only the changed link's pairs miss the cache. Auto-invalidated by Apply / Allocate / Reset / Dismiss.
Toolbar reorganization + floating windowsNetwork toolbar consolidated into three dropdowns: File ▾ (unchanged), 👁 View ▾ (map background + label toggles), ⚡ Automation ▾ (planning + interference + LOS in one menu). Antenna and Radio libraries open as floating, draggable, resizable windows; geometry persisted to localStorage per session.
v8.02026-06-22Tool renamedRenamed to Ceragon Network Planning Tool (was Ceragon Link Budget Calculator in v7.x). Title bar, browser tab, PDF report footer and Pathloss CSV header all carry the new name. The engineering term link budget stays unchanged where it appears as a domain concept (Link Budget Detail card, Apply to Link Budget buttons, PDF / XLSX report titles).
Defaults tabNew top-level mode (third tab) with distance-banded planning rules and global engineering defaults (RSL level, BER threshold, reliability method, misc loss, frame size, ACM/ACAMB). Auto-saves to localStorage.
Network DesignerFull multi-link network planning layer on top of single-link. Bulk site import, sortable Sites & Links tables, dashed-gray drafts vs solid coloured designed links, fitness colour grading.
Quick-Link click-to-connect2 clicks per link (down from 5). Auto-chains forward (B becomes next A). Esc / left-click empty map / right-click cancels.
Network project save / load.lbcnet.json with OS-native file dialog (Chromium), true overwrite-in-place on subsequent saves, named project badge in the toolbar.
Round-trip with Single-LinkBlue context banner with Save to Network / Cancel, pulsing yellow dot for unsaved edits, terrain profile auto-fetched on every link open.
Map layer dropdownSingle control picks basemap from Street (OSM), Satellite (Esri), Terrain (OpenTopoMap), HOT (OSM Humanitarian) or Blank. Same dropdown in both views.
Consolidated toolbarAll file / site / snapshot actions gathered under a single File ▾ dropdown.
Site icon libraryPer-site icons (Generic / Hub / Repeater / Antenna / Customer / POP-BTS / Important / Building) chosen via right-click marker popup. Persisted in saves.
Sticky top barPage header (logo, title, Help / Dark / Export / PDF / File / Clear) and Single Link / Network / Defaults tab strip stay pinned during scroll.
Tab renamed“Single Link” tab → LBC.
Defaults — column reorder + product-driven frequencyColumn order is now Band → Product → Frequency → BW. HP-class products offer 4–11 GHz; standard MW offers 6–42 GHz; mmW frequency is fixed per band.
Defaults — SD columnNew per-row Space Diversity toggle for HP-class products (IP-20C-HP / RFU-D-HP). Planner writes sdEnabled with IFC 3 dB combining and 10 m default separation per side.
Auto reliability parameter on method changePicking Vigants-Barnett → auto C from terrain. ITU-R P.530-7/8 or 17 → auto K from coordinates. P.530-19 → auto K + dN₇₅ from coordinates + altitudes from terrain.
LOS Check toolsNew Network Designer tool with three modes: single-source radius, all-to-all within radius, and tile-bound selection. 60% Fresnel-1 with K=4/3. One-click + Create all draft links from clear candidates. See the LOS Check help section.
Network Chain Mode toggle removedQuick-Link always chains forward; toggle only affected the legacy Add Connection button which now defaults to star / hub-and-spoke.
Right-click cancel fixFixed Leaflet preventDefault bug where the cancel was firing but the native browser context menu covered the toast. Right-click now also dismisses LOS tile mode and LOS results.
Auto-Plan mmW modulation bug fixNaming mismatch (4QAM in MW_CAP vs 4 QAM in MODS) was forcing every E-Band link to BPSK. Planner now picks the correct modulation per BW + configuration.
Auto-Plan LBC load fix (mmW)State snapshot now sets eBandProduct; _lbcSyncDomFromState repopulates the product and Bandwidth dropdowns for the right band. E/W/D-Band auto-planned links open in LBC with the full radio panel populated.
Auto-Plan design shape + draft flagPlanner now writes the proper LBC design wrapper (_format / meta / sites / state) and clears isDraft, so planned links promote from draft to designed and load cleanly when double-clicked.
LBC site marker drag confirmationDragging a placed site marker on the LBC map prompts before committing the move; Cancel snaps the marker back to where the drag started.
Main toolbar File parityMain File ▾ dropdown restructured to mirror Network Designer's sections (Sites · Network file · Single-Link bridge · Clear). Save Network / Save As… / Load Network now sit under their own clear section header. Adds Add Site + Clear Network.
Auto-Plan results contrastFixed near-invisible header text and faded cell values caused by global CSS overrides. Headers now dark navy on a slate-200 band; cells in slate-800.
v7.92026-06-17Update-available bannerPage checks a companion version.txt on load and every 10 min. Shows a banner with one-click cache-busting reload when a newer version is committed.
Cache-control meta tagsno-cache / no-store / must-revalidate added to <head>. Worst-case “old version” window collapses from ~15 min to ~1 min after upload.
Deploy ritualEach release commits both index.html and a one-line version.txt containing the new version string.
v7.8~2026-06-16File menuToolbar consolidated Office-ribbon-style. Single File ▾ dropdown with section headers and per-item file-extension hints. Closes on outside click or Esc.
Import PathlossReads CTE Pathloss V5 (.pl5) and V6 (.pl6) binary link files directly in the browser. Review step before applying.
Pathloss extracted fieldsBand & Ceragon product, frequency, channel BW, configuration, both site names + lat/lon, antenna model + size, antenna heights. Computes hop distance from coords.
Pathloss safe defaultsClimate, rain rate, propagation method, multi-band, ACM/ACMB and diversity settings are never overwritten by import.
Export Pathloss CSVPathloss-compatible CSV (sites + link parameters). Imports into Pathloss V5/V6 via File → Network → Import Network Data.
v7.7~2026-06-16E-Band family expandedFour variants: IP-50E (default, multi-BW with DFM/XPIF baseline), IP-50EX(A), IP-50EX-P (+3 dB TX), IP-50EXA-P (+6 dB TX).
Max Modulation for all bandsExtended from MW-only to E / W / D mmW.
Co-channel XPD / XPIC for mmWExtended from MW-only to all mmW bands (E, W, D) at 2+0; same ITU-R P.530-8 calculation chain.
ACMB filter for IP-50EBPSK 1/4 / BPSK 1/2 hidden when ACMB off; now also applies to IP-50E (previously legacy E-band only).
Multi-Band Band-B syncE-band selector synchronised with the main panel (four variants).
v7.6~2026-06-15IP-50E introducedDefault E-Band product with own 10-BW set (62.5 / 125 / 250 / 500 / 750 / 1000 / 1250 / 1500 / 1750 / 2000 MHz) and per-BW configuration support.
IP-50E datasheet tablesAuthoritative capacity, RSL (Guaranteed BER 10⁻⁶ baseline + offsets for Typical and BER 10⁻³), XPIF and DFM tables wired in. Same XPIF applies uniformly to E/W/D mmW.
Terrain DEM source selectorSix sources: Mapzen (default), Copernicus COP30, ASTER 30 m, EU-DEM 25 m, NED 10 m (USA), SRTM 90 m.
Adaptive DEM samplingOne sample per ~125 m, clamped 60–400, so 30 m DEMs deliver obstruction-detection resolution on short links.
DEM fallback chainChosen DEM → Mapzen → SRTM90 → open-elevation.com → open-meteo.com.
Climate ExplorerClimate override panel with Annual / Month / Day / User-select modes. Primary data NASA POWER with Open-Meteo as fallback.
ITU-R P.530-19 addedLogK + dN₇₅ embedded global grids.
ITU-R P.841 worst-month conversionQ₁=2.85, β=0.13, bounded 1 ≤ Q ≤ 12. Worst-month ↔ annual.
v7.52026-06-15ITU-R P.530-19 multipathEmbedded grids; plus ITU-R P.530-18 frequency-dependent rain path reduction.
Elevation profile resilience4-API fallback chain via Open-Meteo + open-elevation.com.
Ceragon-branded PDF report4-page jsPDF report on-demand. Range Sweep export fix and refreshMaxDist guard.
Multi-Band AggregationMW + mmW or MW + MW with optional dual-band antenna.
Other v7.5 polishparseDMS coordinate input, clear-site buttons, dynamic header subtitle, IP-20C SD eligibility for 6–11 GHz, full FCC unit conversion, band-switch link/profile reset.
Planning estimates only — verify against the equipment datasheet and a field survey before deployment.

💾 Save Link Design

All fields are optional — they're only used for record-keeping inside the saved file and to build a readable filename. The full link configuration (radio, sites, propagation, multi-band) is always saved.
File: LBC_design.json

📥 Import from Pathloss

Review the values extracted from the Pathloss file before applying. Anything missing or shown in amber should be checked against the original — Pathloss has many optional record layouts and not every field is guaranteed to round-trip.
What will be set: band & product, frequency, channel BW, configuration, site A/B coordinates (pins both on the map), antenna heights and antenna sizes. Climate, rain rate, propagation method, multi-band, ACM/ACMB and diversity settings are not overwritten — they keep their current values.
📡 Antenna RPE Library
Drop NSMA file(s) or folder to import
Filter: · size is estimated from gain + midband frequency (D ≈ 10^((G−17.8)/20) / fGHz, 55% efficiency)
Loading library...
Manufacturer Model Freq band Gain Size (est.) BW 3dB Patterns Actions
Select an antenna to view its pattern, or drop NSMA files anywhere on this panel to import.
Format: NSMA WG 16.99.050. Tip: drop a whole folder onto this modal — the entire tree is walked recursively and every .txt/.nsma/.adf/.ant file is imported in one go.
📻 Radio File Library
Drop .raf file(s) or folder to import
Filter:
Loading library...
Product Model Spec Band BW Tx Pwr NF Top mod Actions
Select a radio to view its full specification, or drop .raf files anywhere on this panel.
Format: Pathloss V5 .raf (PL50_ASCII_RADIO_SPEC_04). Each file = one (product, band, channel BW) combo. Contains the spectral mask, selectivity, and T/I curve the interference engine reads directly.
